# Feature Specification: NPC Service
*Generated by Game Studio Sub-Agents v1.0*

## Feature Overview
**Feature Name**: NPC Service — Unified NPC Lifecycle Manager
**Project**: Nookstead
**Engine**: Node.js + Colyseus + Claude API + PostgreSQL + pgvector + Redis
**Priority**: High (Core USP — manages all NPC behavior end-to-end)
**Estimated Effort**: 4 weeks (across M0.2–M0.4)
**Dependencies**: M0.1 (Walking on Tiles), shared types library
**Owner**: Mechanics Developer + Sr Game Designer
**Data Impact**: npc_movement_tick, npc_schedule_action, npc_dialogue_start/turn/end, npc_reflection_generated, npc_plan_generated, npc_service_health

---

## Problem Statement

NPCs are Nookstead's core differentiator. They must feel like living residents: walking between locations on a daily schedule, reacting to the world, holding conversations with players powered by LLM, and remembering past interactions. All of this behavior must be managed by a single, cohesive **NPC Service** — a server-side subsystem that owns the full NPC lifecycle from spawning to daily planning to pathfinding to dialogue to memory.

Currently the GDD describes NPC logic spread across the game server (movement), AI microservice (dialogue), and database (memory). This spec unifies everything under one service architecture with clear module boundaries.

---

## Architecture Overview

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                        NPC SERVICE                          │
│                                                             │
│  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────┐  ┌──────────────────┐  │
│  │  Lifecycle    │  │  Movement    │  │  AI / Dialogue   │  │
│  │  Manager      │  │  Engine      │  │  Engine          │  │
│  │              │  │              │  │                  │  │
│  │ - spawn/     │  │ - A* path-   │  │ - prompt builder │  │
│  │   despawn    │  │   finding    │  │ - Claude API     │  │
│  │ - daily plan │  │ - schedule   │  │ - conversation   │  │
│  │ - tick loop  │  │   executor   │  │   state machine  │  │
│  │ - state FSM  │  │ - collision  │  │ - quick replies  │  │
│  │ - tier mgmt  │  │   avoidance  │  │ - moderation     │  │
│  └──────┬───────┘  └──────┬───────┘  └────────┬─────────┘  │
│         │                 │                    │             │
│  ┌──────┴─────────────────┴────────────────────┴─────────┐  │
│  │                   Memory System                        │  │
│  │                                                       │  │
│  │  - memory stream (write/read)                         │  │
│  │  - importance scoring (Haiku)                         │  │
│  │  - embedding generation                               │  │
│  │  - retrieval (recency + importance + semantic)         │  │
│  │  - reflection (daily synthesis via Sonnet)             │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
│                           │                                  │
│  ┌────────────────────────┴──────────────────────────────┐  │
│  │                   Data Layer                           │  │
│  │  PostgreSQL (npc_agents, memories, reflections)        │  │
│  │  Redis (cached plans, dialogue locks, NPC state)       │  │
│  └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘  │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
         │                    │                    │
    Colyseus Room        Client (Phaser)      Telemetry
    (state sync)         (UI overlay)         (analytics)
```

### Deployment Model

The NPC Service runs **inside the Colyseus game server process** (not as a separate microservice) for Phase 0. This eliminates network latency between game state and NPC logic. The AI/LLM calls are the only external I/O.

```
apps/server/
  src/
    rooms/
      TownRoom.ts                 # Colyseus room — delegates to NPC Service
    npc-service/
      index.ts                    # NPCService class — main entry point
      lifecycle/
        NPCLifecycleManager.ts    # Spawn, despawn, daily plan, tick orchestration
        NPCStateMachine.ts        # Per-NPC state: IDLE, WALKING, WORKING, TALKING, SLEEPING
        NPCTierManager.ts         # FULL / NEARBY / BACKGROUND tier assignment
        DailyPlanner.ts           # Generates daily schedule via Haiku
      movement/
        NPCMovementEngine.ts      # Executes movement commands each tick
        Pathfinder.ts             # A* pathfinding on collision grid
        ScheduleExecutor.ts       # Reads plan, issues move commands at correct times
        CollisionAvoidance.ts     # NPC-NPC and NPC-player collision avoidance
      dialogue/
        DialogueEngine.ts         # Manages conversation lifecycle
        PromptBuilder.ts          # Constructs LLM prompts from persona + context + memories
        ConversationStateMachine.ts  # GREETING → TALKING → FAREWELL → ENDED
        QuickReplyGenerator.ts    # Extracts quick replies from LLM response
        ModerationFilter.ts       # Input toxicity filtering
      memory/
        MemoryStream.ts           # Write new memories to PostgreSQL
        MemoryRetrieval.ts        # Weighted scoring retrieval algorithm
        ImportanceScorer.ts       # Haiku call for 1-10 importance
        EmbeddingService.ts       # Vector embedding generation
        ReflectionEngine.ts       # Daily reflection synthesis via Sonnet
      ai/
        ClaudeClient.ts           # Anthropic SDK wrapper with retry, timeout, cost tracking
        ModelRouter.ts            # Routes to Haiku/Sonnet based on task type
        CostTracker.ts            # Per-NPC, per-player, per-hour cost tracking
      data/
        NPCRepository.ts         # PostgreSQL CRUD for npc_agents
        MemoryRepository.ts      # PostgreSQL CRUD for memories (with pgvector)
        CacheManager.ts          # Redis cache for plans, greetings, state
      types/
        npc-types.ts             # All NPC-related TypeScript types
        events.ts                # NPC event types (for Colyseus messages)
```

---

## Module Specifications

### 1. Lifecycle Manager

Owns the NPC tick loop and orchestrates all other modules.

```typescript
class NPCLifecycleManager {
  private npcs: Map<string, NPCInstance>;
  private tickRate: number = 10; // per second, synced with Colyseus patchRate

  /**
   * Called once when TownRoom is created.
   * Loads all NPC seed personas from DB, spawns NPC instances,
   * generates daily plans for each NPC.
   */
  async initialize(serverId: string): Promise<void>;

  /**
   * Called every server tick (100ms).
   * For each NPC: update state machine, execute movement, check triggers.
   */
  tick(deltaTime: number, gameTime: GameTime): void;

  /**
   * Called once per game-day (every 24 real minutes).
   * Generates new daily plan for each NPC via Haiku.
   * Triggers reflection for each NPC via Sonnet.
   */
  async onNewGameDay(day: number, season: string): Promise<void>;

  /**
   * Called when a player initiates dialogue with an NPC.
   * Validates NPC availability, locks NPC, delegates to DialogueEngine.
   */
  async startDialogue(npcId: string, playerId: string): Promise<DialogueSession>;

  /**
   * Returns NPC state for Colyseus schema sync.
   */
  getNPCStates(): Map<string, NPCStateUpdate>;
}
```

#### NPC State Machine

```
                    ┌──────────┐
         ┌─────────│ SLEEPING │←────────────┐
         │         └──────────┘             │
         │ wake_up (schedule)        sleep (schedule)
         ▼                                  │
    ┌──────────┐  arrive_at    ┌──────────┐ │
    │ WALKING  │──────────────→│ WORKING  │─┘
    │          │←──────────────│          │
    └──────────┘  next_task    └──────────┘
         │                          │
         │ player_interact          │ player_interact
         ▼                          ▼
    ┌──────────┐
    │ TALKING  │ (dialogue session active)
    └──────────┘
         │
         │ dialogue_end
         ▼
    (return to previous state)
```

States:
- **SLEEPING**: NPC is at home, invisible or in bed sprite. No movement. No dialogue. (22:00–06:00)
- **WALKING**: NPC moving along path to next schedule destination. Visible, cannot initiate dialogue mid-step (but can be "caught" within interaction radius).
- **WORKING**: NPC at scheduled location performing activity (baking, shopping, eating). Shows activity icon. Can be talked to.
- **TALKING**: NPC locked in dialogue with a player. Shows speech bubble. Other players see "busy" indicator.
- **IDLE**: Transition state between activities. NPC stands at location, looks around.

#### NPC Tier System

Determines how much CPU/LLM budget an NPC gets based on proximity to players:

| Tier | Condition | Movement | Dialogue | LLM Model | Memory |
|------|-----------|----------|----------|-----------|--------|
| **FULL** | NPC in dialogue OR within 5 tiles of a player | Full A* pathfinding | Available | Sonnet | Full retrieval |
| **NEARBY** | NPC within player viewport (~15 tiles) | Simplified pathfinding (waypoints) | Available | Haiku | Cached only |
| **BACKGROUND** | NPC off-screen from all players | Teleport to next waypoint | Unavailable | None | None |

```typescript
class NPCTierManager {
  /**
   * Recalculates tier for all NPCs based on player positions.
   * Called every 1 second (not every tick — optimization).
   */
  updateTiers(npcs: Map<string, NPCInstance>, players: Map<string, PlayerState>): void;
}
```

---

### 2. Movement Engine

Handles all NPC spatial behavior: pathfinding, schedule execution, collision avoidance.

#### Pathfinding (A*)

```typescript
class Pathfinder {
  private grid: CollisionGrid; // Binary grid from Tiled collision layer

  /**
   * Finds shortest path from start to end, avoiding obstacles.
   * Returns array of tile positions.
   */
  findPath(start: TilePosition, end: TilePosition): TilePosition[];

  /**
   * Updates grid with dynamic obstacles (other NPCs, players).
   * Called before pathfinding if avoidance is enabled.
   */
  updateDynamicObstacles(entities: EntityPosition[]): void;
}
```

- Grid resolution: 1 tile = 1 node (16x16 pixels per node on a 64x64 map = 4096 nodes)
- Algorithm: A* with Manhattan distance heuristic
- Performance target: < 1ms per pathfind on 64x64 grid
- Caching: paths cached per (start, end) pair, invalidated on grid change

#### Schedule Executor

Reads the NPC's daily plan and issues movement commands at the right game-time.

```typescript
interface ScheduleEntry {
  startTime: string;     // "07:00"
  endTime: string;       // "07:30"
  action: string;        // "walk_to_bakery"
  location: TilePosition; // { x: 32, y: 18 }
  activity: string;      // "baking"  (what NPC does on arrival)
  activityIcon: string;  // "bread"   (icon shown above NPC)
}

class ScheduleExecutor {
  /**
   * Called every tick. Checks current game time against schedule.
   * If it's time for next entry, issues walk command to MovementEngine.
   * If NPC arrived at destination, transitions to WORKING state.
   */
  tick(npc: NPCInstance, gameTime: GameTime): MovementCommand | null;
}
```

#### NPC Movement Speed & Animation

- Walk speed: 60 px/sec (slower than player's 100 px/sec — NPCs are leisurely)
- Animation: 4-directional walk cycle, 4 frames each, 8 FPS animation rate
- Idle: facing direction of interest (e.g., towards shop counter when working)

#### Collision Avoidance

NPCs must not overlap each other or players:

- **NPC-NPC**: If two NPCs' paths cross, the one with lower priority (alphabetical ID) pauses for 1 second, then recalculates path
- **NPC-Player**: NPCs path around players. If blocked, NPC waits up to 3 seconds then recalculates
- **Stuck detection**: If NPC hasn't moved for 5 seconds and should be WALKING, teleport to destination (BACKGROUND tier behavior as fallback)

---

### 3. Dialogue Engine

Manages the full conversation lifecycle between a player and an NPC.

#### Conversation State Machine

```
INACTIVE → GREETING → TALKING → FAREWELL → ENDED
                         ↑          │
                         └──────────┘ (if turns remain)
```

```typescript
class DialogueEngine {
  private sessions: Map<string, DialogueSession>; // keyed by npcId

  /**
   * Starts a new dialogue session.
   * Validates NPC is available (not SLEEPING, not already TALKING).
   * Locks NPC (sets inDialogue=true in Colyseus state).
   * Requests initial greeting from AI.
   */
  async startSession(npcId: string, playerId: string, context: DialogueContext): Promise<DialogueSession>;

  /**
   * Processes a player message (quick reply or free text).
   * Runs moderation filter, retrieves memories, builds prompt,
   * calls Claude API, extracts response + emotion + quick replies.
   */
  async processPlayerMessage(sessionId: string, message: string, inputType: 'quick_reply' | 'free_text'): Promise<DialogueResponse>;

  /**
   * Ends the session. NPC says farewell.
   * Unlocks NPC. Stores conversation summary as memory.
   */
  async endSession(sessionId: string, reason: 'turn_limit' | 'player_left' | 'player_closed'): Promise<void>;
}

interface DialogueSession {
  id: string;
  npcId: string;
  playerId: string;
  state: 'greeting' | 'talking' | 'farewell' | 'ended';
  history: DialogueTurn[];
  turnCount: number;
  maxTurns: number;          // 10
  startedAt: Date;
  context: DialogueContext;
}

interface DialogueTurn {
  role: 'player' | 'npc';
  text: string;
  emotion?: string;
  inputType?: 'quick_reply' | 'free_text';
  llmLatencyMs?: number;
  llmCostUsd?: number;
  memoriesUsed?: number;
  timestamp: Date;
}

interface DialogueContext {
  gameTime: GameTime;
  npcCurrentAction: string;
  npcLocation: string;
  weather: string;
  nearbyEntities: string[];   // other players/NPCs within earshot
}
```

#### Prompt Construction

The PromptBuilder assembles the full prompt for Claude:

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ SYSTEM PROMPT                           │
│                                         │
│ 1. Seed Persona (name, traits, bio,     │
│    speech style, profession)            │
│ 2. Current Context (time, location,     │
│    activity, weather)                   │
│ 3. Retrieved Memories (top 10)          │
│ 4. Recent Reflections (top 5)           │
│ 5. Relationship Summary (with player)   │
│ 6. Rules (stay in character, length     │
│    limit, JSON response format)         │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ CONVERSATION HISTORY                    │
│                                         │
│ Player: message 1                       │
│ NPC: response 1                         │
│ Player: message 2                       │
│ ...                                     │
├─────────────────────────────────────────┤
│ RESPONSE FORMAT                         │
│ {                                       │
│   "text": "...",                        │
│   "emotion": "...",                     │
│   "quick_replies": ["...", "...", "..."]│
│ }                                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

Token budget per request:
- System prompt: ~800 tokens
- Memories (10): ~400 tokens
- Reflections (5): ~200 tokens
- Conversation history (up to 10 turns): ~600 tokens
- **Total input**: ~2000 tokens max
- **Output**: ~100 tokens (1-3 sentences + emotion + quick replies)

#### Moderation

```typescript
class ModerationFilter {
  /**
   * Pre-filters player input before sending to LLM.
   * - Keyword blocklist (slurs, explicit content)
   * - Length limit (500 chars max)
   * - Rate limit (1 message per 2 seconds per player)
   *
   * Returns sanitized text or rejection with NPC-in-character response.
   */
  filter(input: string, playerId: string): ModerationResult;
}

type ModerationResult =
  | { ok: true; text: string }
  | { ok: false; npcResponse: string }; // e.g., "Marko looks uncomfortable..."
```

---

### 4. Memory System

Persistent memory that makes NPCs feel alive across sessions.

#### Memory Stream (Write)

Every significant event creates a memory:

| Event | Memory Description Template | Default Importance |
|-------|----------------------------|-------------------|
| Dialogue turn | "Player {name} said: '{summary}'. I responded about {topic}." | Scored by Haiku |
| Gift received | "Player {name} gave me {item}." | 6-8 |
| Player entered shop | "{name} visited my shop at {time}." | 3 |
| Weather change | "It started raining today." | 2 |
| Town event | "The town held {event}. {observation}." | 5-7 |
| NPC-NPC conversation | "I talked to {npc} about {topic}." | 4-6 |

```typescript
class MemoryStream {
  /**
   * Creates a new memory and stores it in PostgreSQL.
   * Generates embedding asynchronously (does not block).
   * Scores importance via Haiku (async, does not block).
   */
  async record(memory: NewMemory): Promise<void>;

  /**
   * Stores a batch of memories (e.g., end-of-dialogue summary).
   */
  async recordBatch(memories: NewMemory[]): Promise<void>;
}

interface NewMemory {
  agentId: string;
  serverId: string;
  gameTimestamp: string;
  description: string;
  memoryType: 'observation' | 'interaction' | 'reflection';
  relatedEntities: string[];
  importanceOverride?: number; // Skip Haiku scoring if provided
}
```

#### Memory Retrieval

```typescript
class MemoryRetrieval {
  /**
   * Retrieves top-K memories for an NPC relevant to a query.
   *
   * Score = α·Recency + β·Importance + γ·Relevance
   *
   * Recency: exponential decay, half-life = 48 game-hours
   * Importance: normalized 0-1 from 1-10 scale
   * Relevance: cosine similarity between query embedding and memory embedding
   *
   * Phase 0 fallback (no embeddings): α=1.0, β=1.0, γ=0.0 (skip semantic search)
   */
  async retrieve(agentId: string, query: string, options?: RetrievalOptions): Promise<RetrievedMemory[]>;
}

interface RetrievalOptions {
  topK: number;            // default: 10
  alpha: number;           // recency weight, default: 1.0
  beta: number;            // importance weight, default: 1.0
  gamma: number;           // relevance weight, default: 1.0
  includeReflections: boolean; // default: true
  maxAgeGameHours: number; // default: unlimited
}
```

#### Reflection Engine

```typescript
class ReflectionEngine {
  /**
   * Called once per game-day by LifecycleManager.onNewGameDay().
   * For each NPC:
   * 1. Fetch top-20 memories from past game-day (by importance)
   * 2. Send to Claude Sonnet with reflection prompt
   * 3. Parse 3-5 insight sentences
   * 4. Store each as a memory with type='reflection', importance=8
   */
  async generateDailyReflections(npc: NPCInstance, gameDay: number): Promise<Reflection[]>;

  /**
   * Batched version — processes all NPCs sequentially with 500ms delay
   * between calls to avoid rate limiting.
   */
  async generateAllReflections(npcs: NPCInstance[], gameDay: number): Promise<void>;
}
```

---

### 5. AI Client Layer

Wraps all LLM interactions with retry logic, cost tracking, and model routing.

```typescript
class ClaudeClient {
  /**
   * Sends a prompt to Claude API.
   * Handles: retry (3 attempts with exponential backoff),
   * timeout (5 seconds), streaming (for dialogue responses).
   */
  async complete(request: LLMRequest): Promise<LLMResponse>;
}

class ModelRouter {
  /**
   * Selects model based on task type and NPC tier.
   */
  route(task: LLMTask, tier: NPCTier): ModelConfig;
}

// Routing rules:
// dialogue (FULL tier)     → Sonnet (streaming)
// dialogue (NEARBY tier)   → Haiku
// daily_plan               → Haiku
// importance_scoring       → Haiku
// reflection               → Sonnet
// reaction                 → Haiku
// npc_conversation         → Haiku
```

#### Cost Tracking

```typescript
class CostTracker {
  /**
   * Records every LLM call with cost breakdown.
   * Emits telemetry events for monitoring.
   */
  record(call: LLMCallRecord): void;

  /**
   * Returns cost summary for a time period.
   */
  getSummary(period: 'hour' | 'day'): CostSummary;
}

interface LLMCallRecord {
  npcId: string;
  playerId?: string;
  task: LLMTask;
  model: 'sonnet' | 'haiku';
  tokensIn: number;
  tokensOut: number;
  costUsd: number;
  latencyMs: number;
  cached: boolean;
}

// Budget targets:
// Per dialogue turn: < $0.007
// Per full dialogue (10 turns): < $0.07
// Per NPC per game-day (plan + reflections): < $0.01
// Per player per real hour: < $0.03
// Per server per real hour (50 NPCs, 20 active players): < $1.00
```

---

### 6. Data Layer

#### PostgreSQL Schema

```sql
-- NPC Agents (seed persona + current state)
CREATE TABLE npc_agents (
  id VARCHAR(64) PRIMARY KEY,           -- 'marko_001'
  server_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
  seed_persona JSONB NOT NULL,          -- Full persona JSON
  current_plan JSONB DEFAULT '[]',      -- Today's schedule entries
  current_state JSONB DEFAULT '{}',     -- FSM state, position, activity
  relationships JSONB DEFAULT '{}',     -- { "player_alex": { summary: "...", sentiment: 0.7 } }
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW(),
  updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

-- Memory Stream
CREATE TABLE memories (
  id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  agent_id VARCHAR(64) REFERENCES npc_agents(id),
  server_id VARCHAR(64) NOT NULL,
  game_timestamp VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL,  -- 'Day 5, 14:30'
  description TEXT NOT NULL,
  importance SMALLINT CHECK (importance BETWEEN 1 AND 10),
  memory_type VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,     -- 'observation', 'interaction', 'reflection'
  related_entities TEXT[] DEFAULT '{}',
  embedding VECTOR(1536),               -- pgvector for semantic search
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_memories_agent ON memories(agent_id);
CREATE INDEX idx_memories_agent_time ON memories(agent_id, created_at DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_memories_importance ON memories(agent_id, importance DESC);
CREATE INDEX idx_memories_type ON memories(agent_id, memory_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_memories_embedding ON memories USING ivfflat (embedding vector_cosine_ops)
  WITH (lists = 100);

-- LLM Cost Log
CREATE TABLE llm_cost_log (
  id BIGSERIAL PRIMARY KEY,
  npc_id VARCHAR(64),
  player_id UUID,
  task VARCHAR(32) NOT NULL,            -- 'dialogue', 'plan', 'reflection', 'importance'
  model VARCHAR(16) NOT NULL,           -- 'sonnet', 'haiku'
  tokens_in INTEGER NOT NULL,
  tokens_out INTEGER NOT NULL,
  cost_usd NUMERIC(8, 6) NOT NULL,
  latency_ms INTEGER NOT NULL,
  cached BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE,
  created_at TIMESTAMPTZ DEFAULT NOW()
);

CREATE INDEX idx_cost_npc ON llm_cost_log(npc_id, created_at);
CREATE INDEX idx_cost_player ON llm_cost_log(player_id, created_at);
```

#### Redis Cache Structure

```
npc:{npcId}:plan         → JSON daily plan (TTL: 24 game-minutes = 24 real minutes)
npc:{npcId}:state        → JSON current FSM state (TTL: none, updated every tick)
npc:{npcId}:greeting:{hour} → Cached greeting for this hour (TTL: 1 game-hour = 1 real minute)
npc:{npcId}:dialogue_lock → Player ID who has dialogue lock (TTL: 5 minutes safety)
npc:{npcId}:tier         → Current tier assignment (TTL: 5 seconds)
npc:reflection_queue     → List of NPC IDs pending reflection (consumed by ReflectionEngine)
```

---

## Integration with Colyseus

The NPC Service is instantiated inside `TownRoom`:

```typescript
// apps/server/src/rooms/TownRoom.ts
import { Room, Client } from '@colyseus/core';
import { WorldState } from './schemas/WorldState';
import { NPCService } from '../npc-service';

export class TownRoom extends Room<WorldState> {
  private npcService: NPCService;

  async onCreate(options: any) {
    this.setState(new WorldState());

    this.npcService = new NPCService({
      serverId: this.roomId,
      collisionGrid: this.loadCollisionGrid(),
      db: this.getDbConnection(),
      redis: this.getRedisConnection(),
    });

    await this.npcService.initialize();

    // Sync NPC states to Colyseus schema every tick
    this.setSimulationInterval((deltaTime) => {
      this.npcService.tick(deltaTime, this.getGameTime());
      this.syncNPCStates();
    }, 100); // 10 ticks/sec

    // Handle dialogue messages from clients
    this.onMessage('dialogue_start', (client, data) => {
      this.npcService.startDialogue(data.npcId, client.sessionId);
    });

    this.onMessage('dialogue_message', (client, data) => {
      this.npcService.processDialogueMessage(data.sessionId, data.message, data.inputType);
    });

    this.onMessage('dialogue_end', (client, data) => {
      this.npcService.endDialogue(data.sessionId, 'player_closed');
    });
  }

  private syncNPCStates() {
    const states = this.npcService.getNPCStates();
    for (const [id, state] of states) {
      const npc = this.state.npcs.get(id);
      if (npc) {
        npc.position.x = state.x;
        npc.position.y = state.y;
        npc.animation = state.animation;
        npc.currentAction = state.activityIcon;
        npc.emotion = state.emotion;
        npc.inDialogue = state.inDialogue;
      }
    }
  }
}
```

---

## Seed Persona Format

Each NPC is defined by a seed persona JSON stored in `npc_agents.seed_persona`:

```json
{
  "name": "Марко",
  "name_en": "Marko",
  "age": 38,
  "profession": "Пекарь",
  "traits": ["экстраверт", "щедрый", "импульсивный", "креативный", "тревожный"],
  "bio": "Марко переехал в Тихую Гавань 5 лет назад из большого города, где работал в ресторане. Всегда мечтал о своей пекарне. Открыл лавку на Торговой улице и гордится каждой буханкой. Боится, что бизнес не выживет, если появятся конкуренты.",
  "interests": ["выпечка", "футбол", "громкая музыка"],
  "fears": ["банкротство", "одиночество"],
  "goals": ["расширить пекарню", "найти ученика"],
  "speech_style": "Громкий, эмоциональный, часто использует восклицания. Постоянно предлагает попробовать свежий хлеб.",
  "daily_routine": {
    "06:00": { "action": "wake_up", "location": "home" },
    "06:30": { "action": "walk_to_bakery", "location": "bakery" },
    "07:00": { "action": "baking", "location": "bakery", "icon": "bread" },
    "12:00": { "action": "walk_to_cafe", "location": "cafe" },
    "12:30": { "action": "eating_lunch", "location": "cafe", "icon": "food" },
    "13:00": { "action": "walk_to_bakery", "location": "bakery" },
    "13:30": { "action": "selling", "location": "bakery", "icon": "coins" },
    "18:00": { "action": "walk_to_bar", "location": "bar" },
    "18:30": { "action": "socializing", "location": "bar", "icon": "beer" },
    "21:00": { "action": "walk_home", "location": "home" },
    "22:00": { "action": "sleeping", "location": "home" }
  },
  "locations": {
    "home": { "x": 10, "y": 45 },
    "bakery": { "x": 32, "y": 18 },
    "cafe": { "x": 28, "y": 22 },
    "bar": { "x": 40, "y": 25 }
  }
}
```

---

## Performance Constraints

| Metric | Target | Method |
|--------|--------|--------|
| NPC tick processing (50 NPCs) | < 5ms per tick | Tier system reduces work for off-screen NPCs |
| A* pathfinding (64x64 grid) | < 1ms per call | Grid caching, path caching |
| Dialogue LLM response | < 3s (p50), < 5s (p95) | Streaming, Sonnet for FULL tier |
| Memory retrieval (1000 memories) | < 200ms | pgvector IVFFlat index |
| Importance scoring | < 500ms | Async, does not block dialogue |
| Daily plan generation | < 1s per NPC | Haiku, cached in Redis |
| Daily reflections (50 NPCs) | < 5 minutes total | Sequential with 500ms delays |
| Redis state updates | < 1ms per NPC | Pipelining |
| Total LLM cost per server-hour | < $1.00 | Tier system + caching |

---

## Balancing Parameters

| Parameter | Default | Range | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|-------|---------|
| NPC walk speed | 60 px/sec | 40-80 | Leisurely pace, slower than player |
| FULL tier radius | 5 tiles | 3-8 | How close before NPC gets full AI |
| NEARBY tier radius | 15 tiles | 10-20 | Viewport-approximate range |
| Max dialogue turns | 10 | 6-15 | Cost vs engagement balance |
| Max response length | 150 chars | 80-250 | Game-appropriate brevity |
| Quick reply count | 3 | 2-4 | Choice vs cognitive load |
| Memory retrieval K | 10 | 5-20 | Context richness vs token cost |
| Recency half-life | 48 game-hours | 24-96 | How fast old memories fade |
| Reflection frequency | 1/game-day | 1-2/game-day | Quality vs cost |
| Stuck teleport timeout | 5 sec | 3-10 | Patience before teleport fallback |
| Collision avoidance pause | 1 sec | 0.5-2 | NPC-NPC path conflict resolution |

---

## Edge Cases & Error Handling

| Scenario | Behavior |
|----------|----------|
| NPC already in dialogue | Show speech bubble "Занят разговором" to other players |
| Player walks away mid-dialogue | Auto-close session after 3 seconds out of range. NPC says abbreviated farewell |
| LLM timeout (>5s) | Return cached generic response: "Простите, задумался. Что вы говорили?" |
| LLM error/failure | Use cached greeting. Log error. Retry on next turn |
| Toxic player input | Moderation filter rejects. NPC responds in-character: "{Name} выглядит некомфортно и меняет тему" |
| NPC stuck (no movement for 5s) | Teleport to next waypoint. Log npc_stuck event |
| Pathfinding fails (no path) | NPC stays at current position, logs warning. Try again next tick |
| Multiple players approach NPC | First player gets dialogue. Others see "busy" indicator |
| Server restart mid-dialogue | Session lost. NPC resets to schedule. No memory of interrupted conversation |
| Memory DB write fails | Queue for retry via Redis. Do not block dialogue flow |
| Redis unavailable | Fall back to in-memory cache. Degrade gracefully |
| NPC daily plan generation fails | Use previous day's plan with randomized ±30min offsets |
| Cost budget exceeded ($1/hr) | Switch all NPCs to Haiku. Alert via telemetry. Reduce reflection frequency |

---

## Analytics Integration

### Telemetry Events

| Event | Fields | Frequency |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| `npc_service_health` | active_npcs, tiers_breakdown, tick_processing_ms, memory_count | Every 60s |
| `npc_movement_tick` | npc_id, from, to, path_length, pathfind_ms | On path start |
| `npc_schedule_action` | npc_id, action, location, game_time | On schedule transition |
| `npc_dialogue_start` | npc_id, player_id, npc_state, game_time | On dialogue open |
| `npc_dialogue_turn` | npc_id, player_id, turn_number, input_type, llm_model, latency_ms, tokens, cost, memories_retrieved | Each turn |
| `npc_dialogue_end` | npc_id, player_id, total_turns, total_cost, duration_s, end_reason | On dialogue close |
| `npc_memory_created` | agent_id, memory_type, importance, related_entities | On memory write |
| `npc_reflection_generated` | npc_id, game_day, reflection_count, memories_processed, cost | On reflection |
| `npc_plan_generated` | npc_id, game_day, entries_count, cost | On daily plan |
| `npc_stuck` | npc_id, position, target, duration_s | On stuck detection |
| `npc_cost_alert` | server_id, hourly_cost, threshold | On budget exceeded |

### Key Dashboards

1. **NPC Health**: Tier distribution, stuck events, movement completion rate
2. **Dialogue Quality**: Turns per session, free text ratio, session duration, repeat conversation rate
3. **LLM Costs**: Per-NPC, per-player, per-hour breakdowns. Model usage split. Cache hit rate
4. **Memory System**: Growth rate, retrieval relevance scores, reflection quality (manual sampling)

---

## Testing Plan

### Unit Tests
- [ ] NPCStateMachine transitions correctly between all states
- [ ] Pathfinder returns valid path on test grid
- [ ] Pathfinder returns empty array when no path exists
- [ ] ScheduleExecutor issues correct commands at correct game-times
- [ ] NPCTierManager assigns tiers correctly based on distances
- [ ] DialogueEngine enforces turn limits
- [ ] DialogueEngine rejects dialogue when NPC is SLEEPING or TALKING
- [ ] PromptBuilder assembles prompt within token budget
- [ ] ModerationFilter catches blocked keywords
- [ ] ModerationFilter enforces rate limit
- [ ] MemoryStream records memories with correct fields
- [ ] MemoryRetrieval returns top-K by combined score
- [ ] ImportanceScorer returns value 1-10
- [ ] CostTracker accumulates costs correctly
- [ ] ModelRouter selects correct model for each task+tier combo

### Integration Tests
- [ ] Full NPC lifecycle: spawn → plan → walk → work → dialogue → memory → reflection
- [ ] Dialogue flow: player interact → greeting → 3 turns → farewell → memory stored
- [ ] Memory persistence: talk to NPC → restart server → talk again → NPC references past conversation
- [ ] Multi-player: Player A talks to NPC while Player B sees "busy" indicator
- [ ] Tier transitions: player walks toward NPC → tier upgrades → NPC gets full AI
- [ ] Schedule execution: NPC follows daily routine for a full game-day

### Performance Tests
- [ ] 50 NPCs tick processing < 5ms (no players)
- [ ] 50 NPCs + 20 players tick processing < 15ms
- [ ] A* pathfinding < 1ms on 64x64 grid
- [ ] Memory retrieval < 200ms with 1000 memories
- [ ] Dialogue response < 3s (p50) with Sonnet
- [ ] Daily reflections for 50 NPCs < 5 minutes
- [ ] Total LLM cost for 1-hour simulation < $1.00

### Player Tests
- [ ] NPC movement feels natural (not robotic, not teleporting)
- [ ] NPC appears to "live" their daily life (seen at bakery in morning, cafe at lunch)
- [ ] Dialogue feels in-character and contextually appropriate
- [ ] NPC references past interactions naturally (the "wow" moment)
- [ ] NPC tier transitions are invisible to the player (no visible pop-in or behavior change)

---

## Implementation Roadmap

| Phase | Milestone | NPC Service Work |
|-------|-----------|-----------------|
| M0.2 | Multiplayer Sync | Lifecycle Manager (spawn, tick loop), Movement Engine (pathfinding, schedule executor), Tier Manager (basic), Colyseus integration |
| M0.3 | NPC Talks | Dialogue Engine (full), Prompt Builder, Claude Client, Moderation Filter, Cost Tracker |
| M0.4 | NPC Remembers | Memory Stream, Memory Retrieval, Importance Scorer, Embedding Service, Reflection Engine |

All modules live in `apps/server/src/npc-service/` from day one. Each milestone adds modules to the service without refactoring existing ones.

---

## Future Considerations (Phase 1+)

- **NPC-to-NPC conversations**: Two NPCs meet and have Haiku-powered dialogue visible to nearby players
- **Dynamic daily plans**: Replace static schedule with LLM-generated plans influenced by yesterday's events
- **NPC reactions**: NPCs react to world events in real-time (weather, festivals, player actions)
- **Relationship model**: Numeric relationship scores + narrative summaries per player
- **NPC emotions**: Persistent emotional state that influences dialogue tone
- **Multiple servers**: NPC personas shared, but memories and relationships are per-server
- **NPC migration**: Move NPC Service to separate microservice when server count > 5

---

## Agent Approvals
*Required sign-offs before implementation*

- [ ] **Market Analyst**: NPC service is the core USP — architecture must support quality and cost targets
- [ ] **Sr Game Designer**: NPC behavior (schedule, movement, dialogue) creates believable residents
- [ ] **Mechanics Developer**: Architecture is implementable within Colyseus, performance targets achievable
- [ ] **Data Scientist**: Telemetry covers all cost, quality, and engagement metrics
- [ ] **QA Agent**: Testing plan covers lifecycle, dialogue, memory, and performance
- [ ] **Producer Agent**: Implementation roadmap aligns with milestone timeline

**Final Approval**: [ ] **Master Orchestrator** - NPC Service specification approved for development
